sub_criteria.name: Common Characteristics of Good Applications
sub_criteria.definition: The application demonstrates characteristics commonly found in strong grant applications.

signals.signal_text: Clear research vision and career trajectory
Distinctive ideas with an independent direction
Well-justified training plan
Strong mentorship and institutional support

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly articulates a vision to become a future leader in evidence synthesis for women's health, with a structured training plan and strong supervisory and institutional support.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The distinctiveness of the research idea (g.1.b) could be more explicitly highlighted; the training plan (g.1.c) lists many courses but lacks explicit justification for each.
sub_criteria.rationale: The distinctiveness of the research idea (g.1.b) could be more explicitly highlighted; the training plan (g.1.c) lists many courses but lacks explicit justification for each.

sub_criteria.name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award
sub_criteria.definition: The applicant clearly articulates why this specific award is the right next step in their career.
sub_criteria.group_name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award

signals.signal_text: Explain why this award is the right next step in your career (not just another project grant)
What difference would securing an award make to your career, and why now?
How will an award build on your existing experience and what new skills will it enable you to gain?
How will it develop your research partnerships within the NHS, public health or social care, charity organisations, industry or other organisations?

sub_criteria.pros: Strong reasoning for the fellowship as the next step, building on existing experience and enabling new skills; the work placement at MHRA fosters collaborations.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The urgency ('why now') is implied but not explicitly stated; partnerships with NHS or public health organisations are not directly mentioned.
sub_criteria.rationale: The urgency ('why now') is implied but not explicitly stated; partnerships with NHS or public health organisations are not directly mentioned.

sub_criteria.name: Relevant Research Experience and Training
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates prior research experience and/or training that prepares the applicant to successfully undertake the proposed award in applied health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of previous projects, research methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study
Evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications
Alignment between prior experience and skills required for the proposed project

sub_criteria.pros: Extensive prior experience in systematic reviews, meta-analysis, and trial management directly relevant to the proposed project.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Formal prior training (g.3.b) is not detailed in the provided text; the listed courses are mostly planned rather than completed.
sub_criteria.rationale: Formal prior training (g.3.b) is not detailed in the provided text; the listed courses are mostly planned rather than completed.

sub_criteria.name: Impactful Research Outputs and Outcomes
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful research outputs or outcomes appropriate to the applicant’s career stage, demonstrating contribution to the field.

signals.signal_text: Number and quality of publications, reports, or presentations
Citation metrics or other indicators of impact (where mentioned)
Awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research
Description of practical or societal impact of previous work

sub_criteria.pros: First-authorship of a Cochrane review and a conference presentation demonstrate high-quality outputs.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No citation metrics, awards, or specific practical impact examples are provided; the societal benefit is stated in general terms.
sub_criteria.rationale: No citation metrics, awards, or specific practical impact examples are provided; the societal benefit is stated in general terms.

sub_criteria.name: Trajectory Towards Research Leadership
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates potential and progression towards becoming a future leader in health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Statements of career ambitions and long-term goals
Evidence of increasing responsibility in previous roles (e.g., leading projects, mentoring)
Participation in leadership or professional development programs
Recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential

sub_criteria.pros: Clear career ambition to become a leader, with evidence of increasing responsibility through leading manuscripts and invited co-authorship; leadership training is included.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Recognition (g.5.d) is limited to one invitation and director's statement; leadership program participation is only one course.
sub_criteria.rationale: Recognition (g.5.d) is limited to one invitation and director's statement; leadership program participation is only one course.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Practitioner-Academic Career (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: For applicants including a clinical or practice element, shows a clear commitment to combining research with professional practice.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions
Involvement in clinical or practice-based research
Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities
Participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications

sub_criteria.drawbacks: No evidence of a practitioner-academic career; the applicant is a researcher without clinical practice involvement.
sub_criteria.rationale: No evidence of a practitioner-academic career; the applicant is a researcher without clinical practice involvement.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of the Plain English Summary
sub_criteria.definition: Clear, simple description of the research that a non-specialist audience can understand; explains problem, importance, plan, beneficiaries, and real-world use; avoids jargon; aligns with the detailed proposal.

signals.signal_text: Readability scores (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid)
Low density of unexplained jargon or technical terms
Presence of problem statement, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and real-world impact
Alignment with detailed proposal content
Sentence structure and coherence for non-specialist understanding
Emphasis on societal or patient relevance

sub_criteria.pros: The Plain English Summary effectively covers all required components (problem, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, impact) and uses plain language with good readability scores (Flesch-Kincaid grade 8.79, reading ease 62.73). It emphasizes patient and societal relevance and aligns well with the detailed proposal.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Some technical terms such as 'meta-analysis' may require further explanation for a non-specialist audience, and the raw summary text was not directly available for detailed coherence assessment. The lexical overlap with the detailed proposal is moderate, suggesting potential gaps in alignment.
sub_criteria.rationale: Some technical terms such as 'meta-analysis' may require further explanation for a non-specialist audience, and the raw summary text was not directly available for detailed coherence assessment. The lexical overlap with the detailed proposal is moderate, suggesting potential gaps in alignment.

sub_criteria.name: Evidence Review Quality & Relevance
sub_criteria.definition: How comprehensively and critically the application reviews existing literature and evidence.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of references cited
Presence of critical analysis vs. mere summary
Identification of knowledge gaps
Coherence and logical flow

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides a comprehensive and critical review of the existing evidence, clearly identifying key knowledge gaps in HMB measurement and meta-analysis. References are relevant and up-to-date.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The total number of references is not explicitly enumerated, and some sections may benefit from more detailed discussion of prior systematic reviews.
sub_criteria.rationale: The total number of references is not explicitly enumerated, and some sections may benefit from more detailed discussion of prior systematic reviews.

sub_criteria.name: Research Scope & Award Appropriateness
sub_criteria.definition: Whether the project scope matches the scale and expectations of the proposed award (doctoral, postdoctoral, etc.).

signals.signal_text: Alignment of aims with award level
Feasibility indicators (time, complexity, tasks)
Proportion of work relative to award expectations

sub_criteria.pros: The research is structured into three well-defined work packages with clear timelines (30 months), demonstrating feasibility and appropriate scope for a fellowship award.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Details on contingency plans or risk mitigation are not explicitly discussed, and the complexity of multivariate meta-analysis may require additional training.
sub_criteria.rationale: Details on contingency plans or risk mitigation are not explicitly discussed, and the complexity of multivariate meta-analysis may require additional training.

sub_criteria.name: Research Design & Methodological Rigor
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity, appropriateness, and robustness of study design to answer the research questions.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of methods
Logical alignment between aims and methods
Discussion of controls, sample size, or methodology justification
Structured methodological sections

sub_criteria.pros: The research design includes robust methods such as systematic review with COSMIN appraisal, multivariate meta-analysis, and qualitative thematic analysis, clearly aligned with the stated aims.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Sample size justification for the qualitative component is not explicitly provided, and the methodological details could be more extensively elaborated in the proposal.
sub_criteria.rationale: Sample size justification for the qualitative component is not explicitly provided, and the methodological details could be more extensively elaborated in the proposal.

sub_criteria.name: Potential Impact on Patients, Public, and Health Services
sub_criteria.definition: Likely benefits or influence of the research on patients, carers, or health and care systems.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of direct or indirect patient/public benefits
Alignment with health priorities or societal needs
Clarity and specificity of expected outcomes

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly outlines direct patient benefits including improved quality of life and reduced economic burden, and aligns with national health priorities for HMB. Expected outcomes are well-specified.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Quantified impact indicators (e.g., projected reduction in waiting times) are not provided, and the dissemination plan could include more concrete metrics for reach.
sub_criteria.rationale: Quantified impact indicators (e.g., projected reduction in waiting times) are not provided, and the dissemination plan could include more concrete metrics for reach.

sub_criteria.name: Engagement with People & Communities
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful involvement of patients, carers, or communities.

signals.signal_text: Use of co-design, participatory methods, or consultations
References to ethical considerations and feedback mechanisms
Description of involvement at multiple project stages

sub_criteria.pros: The proposal demonstrates extensive engagement with patients and the public, including a dedicated PPI committee that will guide the research across all work packages. Prior consultation has already shaped the proposal.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Ethical considerations and formal feedback mechanisms are not explicitly detailed, and the plan could be strengthened by outlining how PPI will be evaluated.
sub_criteria.rationale: Ethical considerations and formal feedback mechanisms are not explicitly detailed, and the plan could be strengthened by outlining how PPI will be evaluated.

sub_criteria.name: Justification of Resources & Award Costs
sub_criteria.definition: Appropriateness and clarity of requested resources for the planned work.

signals.signal_text: Explicit links between tasks and resources requested
Budget proportionality to project scope
Clear rationale for funding level

sub_criteria.pros: The budget provides detailed justifications for all items, with clear links to project tasks and a proportionate allocation for the scope of work.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The rationale for the overall funding level is not explicitly discussed, and without the full budget section, it is difficult to assess completeness.
sub_criteria.rationale: The rationale for the overall funding level is not explicitly discussed, and without the full budget section, it is difficult to assess completeness.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of Previous Research (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: Prior relevant research experience demonstrating capability to deliver the proposed project.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of publications or outputs
Evidence of prior successful projects
Skills alignment with proposed work

sub_criteria.pros: The applicant has relevant prior research experience, including a first-author Cochrane review and multiple PROSPERO protocols, demonstrating capability to deliver the proposed evidence synthesis work.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The number of publications is limited, and direct experience with multivariate meta-analysis is not explicitly demonstrated in the background.
sub_criteria.rationale: The number of publications is limited, and direct experience with multivariate meta-analysis is not explicitly demonstrated in the background.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusive Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: Consideration of inclusivity throughout the research lifecycle, including participant diversity and accessibility.

signals.signal_text: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment
Adaptive methods or accessibility considerations
Equity and participation indicators

sub_criteria.pros: The proposal includes explicit strategies to recruit a diverse sample by age, ethnicity, and region, using translators to reduce barriers. The PPI committee is also planned to be diverse.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Specific equity indicators or monitoring plans for recruitment diversity are not detailed, and accessibility considerations beyond translation are limited.
sub_criteria.rationale: Specific equity indicators or monitoring plans for recruitment diversity are not detailed, and accessibility considerations beyond translation are limited.

sub_criteria.name: Quality and Relevance of Proposed Training
sub_criteria.definition: How well-structured, clear, and relevant the training plan is in relation to the project’s goals and the applicant’s research needs.

signals.signal_text: Completeness and clarity of training description
Alignment of training topics with project methods or techniques
Structured timeline or sequencing of training activities
Explicit links between training content and skill acquisition

sub_criteria.pros: Provides a comprehensive and clear description of training, including specific courses aligned to work package content, use of a Skills Evaluation Tool, and mapping to Researcher Development Framework. The Gantt chart (appendix) provides a timeline.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The timeline is not fully detailed in the main text; some courses are generic (e.g., 'Effective Presentations'), though still relevant.
sub_criteria.rationale: The timeline is not fully detailed in the main text; some courses are generic (e.g., 'Effective Presentations'), though still relevant.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability to Personal Development and Project Needs
sub_criteria.definition: How well the proposed training addresses the applicant’s current skills gaps and supports successful project delivery.

signals.signal_text: Identification of personal skill gaps
Clear mapping between training activities and project tasks
Evidence that proposed training is targeted rather than generic
Mention of mentorship, workshops, or formal courses relevant to development needs

sub_criteria.pros: Personal skill gaps are identified via a Skills Evaluation Tool; training activities are clearly mapped to project tasks (e.g., search strategies for WP1, qualitative courses for WP3). Mentorship, formal courses, and research visits are included.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Skill gaps are not explicitly listed; some training (e.g., 'Networking skills') is generic, though still beneficial.
sub_criteria.rationale: Skill gaps are not explicitly listed; some training (e.g., 'Networking skills') is generic, though still beneficial.

sub_criteria.name: Support for Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: The extent to which the training plan, and any professional practice components, contribute to the applicant’s long-term career goals.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of career goals
Connections between training activities and future career trajectory
Indications of skill transferability beyond the current project
Reference to professional practice or experiential learning where applicable

sub_criteria.pros: Explicit career goal to become a leader in evidence synthesis and women's health; training activities (conferences, research visits, work placements) directly support this trajectory. Includes experiential learning through visits and placements.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Connections between specific training and long-term career could be more detailed; skill transferability is mentioned but not fully explored.
sub_criteria.rationale: Connections between specific training and long-term career could be more detailed; skill transferability is mentioned but not fully explored.

sub_criteria.name: Track Record of the Contracting Organisation (and Partners)
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrated expertise, experience, and research achievements of the organisation(s) in the proposed research area.

signals.signal_text: Publication or project history in relevant research fields
Previous funding success or awards in similar domains
Mention of prior collaborations or partnerships
Highlighted achievements or notable outputs

sub_criteria.pros: The director's statement confirms that the applicant is embedded in the Meta-Analysis Programme at MRC CTU at UCL, indicating an environment with relevant research focus.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No explicit evidence of the organisation's publication history, prior funding success, collaborations, or notable outputs. The track record is only implied by the director's support and the existence of the programme.
sub_criteria.rationale: No explicit evidence of the organisation's publication history, prior funding success, collaborations, or notable outputs. The track record is only implied by the director's support and the existence of the programme.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability of Supervisory Support and Mentorship Arrangements
sub_criteria.definition: Quality, clarity, and appropriateness of supervision and mentoring for supporting the applicant’s research and career development.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of supervisor roles and responsibilities
Evidence of mentorship plans, meetings, or training support
Alignment between supervisor expertise and project needs
Presence of structured supervision framework (frequency, format, accountability)

sub_criteria.pros: Supervisor roles, expertise, and meeting frequency are explicitly described for multiple supervisors, with clear alignment to project needs (qualitative work, statistics, meta-analysis). The supervision framework is structured with monthly meetings and ongoing communication.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Some supervisors' descriptions are more detailed than others; for example, Supervisor 1 (not shown) may have less detail. Overall, very strong.
sub_criteria.rationale: Some supervisors' descriptions are more detailed than others; for example, Supervisor 1 (not shown) may have less detail. Overall, very strong.

sub_criteria.name: Appropriateness of the Contracting Organisation to Support the Award and Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: How well the organisation (and any partners) can provide the infrastructure, resources, and environment necessary to deliver the project and support the applicant’s career growth.

signals.signal_text: Description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided
Links between organisational support and project success
Alignment of organisational focus with applicant’s career trajectory
Evidence of tailored support for award type (doctoral, postdoctoral)

sub_criteria.pros: The host organisation (MRC CTU at UCL) provides a supportive environment with the Meta-Analysis Programme, a doctoral training context, and networking opportunities as indicated by the director's statement and belief state. The organisation's focus on clinical trials and meta-analysis aligns well with the applicant's career goals.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Specific details on facilities, infrastructure, and resources are not explicitly described in the provided text; the evidence relies on the director's general statement and the belief state.
sub_criteria.rationale: Specific details on facilities, infrastructure, and resources are not explicitly described in the provided text; the evidence relies on the director's general statement and the belief state.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Inclusive and Supportive Research Culture
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that the organisation fosters inclusivity, research integrity, and a supportive environment for all researchers.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion
Evidence of inclusive recruitment, mentoring, or support initiatives
References to adherence to research integrity principles
Statements on organisational culture, training, or initiatives supporting underrepresented groups

sub_criteria.pros: UCL and ICTM are cited as holding Athena Swan awards and Race Equality Charter, with mandated EDI training, demonstrating a strong commitment to inclusive research culture.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No explicit mention of research integrity principles or specific support initiatives for underrepresented groups in the provided text, but the awards and policies are strong indicators.
sub_criteria.rationale: No explicit mention of research integrity principles or specific support initiatives for underrepresented groups in the provided text, but the awards and policies are strong indicators.

sub_criteria.name: Integration of Lived Experience in Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: How input from patients, carers, or people with relevant lived experience has shaped the research questions, design, and methods.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of consultation or co-design activities
Specific examples of changes made to study design based on lived experience
References to patient or community advisory groups

sub_criteria.pros: Clear evidence of prior and planned consultation/co-design activities (e.g., co-creating adverts, interviews); specific example of changing from focus groups to interviews based on feedback; established PPI committee.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Only one concrete example of design change; further detail on how lived experience shaped specific methods would strengthen.
sub_criteria.rationale: Only one concrete example of design change; further detail on how lived experience shaped specific methods would strengthen.

sub_criteria.name: Diversity and Inclusivity of the Research Team
sub_criteria.definition: Extent to which the research team reflects diversity in terms of skills, experience, and demographic representation.

signals.signal_text: Explicit mentions of team composition across gender, ethnicity, age, or other relevant characteristics
Statements about inclusion in hiring or team-building
Evidence of equitable task allocation and collaboration

sub_criteria.pros: Mentions of prior multidisciplinary team including Indigenous representatives, but this does not address the proposed research team.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No explicit description of team composition (gender, ethnicity, etc.) for the proposed project; no statements about inclusive hiring or equitable collaboration.
sub_criteria.rationale: No explicit description of team composition (gender, ethnicity, etc.) for the proposed project; no statements about inclusive hiring or equitable collaboration.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Maximising Public and Patient Benefit
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity and strength of the plan to ensure research outcomes meaningfully benefit patients, carers, and the public.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of dissemination, knowledge translation, or implementation plans
Clear links between study objectives and anticipated benefits
Emphasis on actionable outcomes or improvements in health/care

sub_criteria.pros: Comprehensive dissemination plan (conferences, papers, social media, blogs); clear link between study objectives and improved patient outcomes; emphasis on actionable findings for guidelines.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Specificity of actionable outcomes could be enhanced with more concrete implementation steps.
sub_criteria.rationale: Specificity of actionable outcomes could be enhanced with more concrete implementation steps.

sub_criteria.name: Representation of Seldom Heard or Underserved Groups
sub_criteria.definition: Degree to which participants and PPI contributors represent groups historically underrepresented in research, including those facing health inequalities.

signals.signal_text: Mention of specific demographic groups (age, race, ethnicity, disability, gender, sexual orientation, religion/belief, location, socioeconomic status)
Evidence of targeted recruitment strategies
Discussion of barriers to participation and mitigation strategies

sub_criteria.pros: Explicit mention of underrepresented groups (Black women, diverse ages/ethnicities); targeted recruitment via purposive sampling and translators; awareness of cultural barriers.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Detailed strategies for barrier mitigation could be expanded; no specific outreach to groups like disabled or LGBTQ+ women.
sub_criteria.rationale: Detailed strategies for barrier mitigation could be expanded; no specific outreach to groups like disabled or LGBTQ+ women.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusivity Across the Research Lifecycle
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstration that inclusion is considered at all stages of the project: planning, recruitment, conduct, analysis, and dissemination.

signals.signal_text: References to inclusion in study design, participant selection, and analysis
Consideration of accessibility and equity in all project stages
Documented plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation

sub_criteria.pros: Clear involvement of PPI across multiple stages: design, recruitment, data collection, dissemination, and evaluation.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Accessibility considerations are only partially addressed (online/in-person, translators); no explicit plan for ongoing evaluation or adaptive measures.
sub_criteria.rationale: Accessibility considerations are only partially addressed (online/in-person, translators); no explicit plan for ongoing evaluation or adaptive measures.

sub_criteria.name: Costed and Resourced Plan for Research Inclusion
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that research inclusion has been properly budgeted and resourced within the proposal.

signals.signal_text: Explicit budget line(s) for inclusion activities (PPI, outreach, recruitment support)
Mention of staff time, materials, or training dedicated to inclusion
Alignment between inclusion plan and allocated resources

sub_criteria.pros: Budget includes explicit lines for PPI activities (reimbursements, travel, training); resources align with planned activities.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Detail on staff time allocation for inclusion tasks could be more precise; training costs are mentioned but not fully itemized.
sub_criteria.rationale: Detail on staff time allocation for inclusion tasks could be more precise; training costs are mentioned but not fully itemized.

sub_criteria.name: Use of Formatting, Headings, and Subheadings
sub_criteria.definition: Effective use of text formatting to make the application clear, navigable, and easy to read.

signals.signal_text: Presence and consistency of headings and subheadings
Use of bold or emphasis for key terms
Structured hierarchy of sections (logical nesting of headings)
Patterns detected via text parsing for formatting markers

sub_criteria.pros: The application uses a clear section structure with 10 detected sections and 8 numbered heading-like lines, plus 45 bullet/numbered list markers and 22 emphasis markers, indicating effective formatting.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Use of bold/emphasis is partially detected due to parser limitations (only 22 markers found), so signal af.1.b is only moderate. Some sections may lack subheadings, but overall hierarchy is evident.
sub_criteria.rationale: Use of bold/emphasis is partially detected due to parser limitations (only 22 markers found), so signal af.1.b is only moderate. Some sections may lack subheadings, but overall hierarchy is evident.

sub_criteria.name: Limited Duplication and Repetition
sub_criteria.definition: Avoidance of unnecessary repetition within or across sections.

signals.signal_text: Semantic similarity measures between sentences/paragraphs
Detection of repeated phrases or ideas
Novelty score for each section (unique content vs. overlap)

sub_criteria.pros: No duplicate sentences or repeated lines are detected (duplicate_sentence_rate_excluding_budget=0.0, repeated_line_rate=0.0). Cross-section lexical overlaps are moderate (max 0.26), indicating good novelty.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While duplication is minimal, some lexical overlap between sections exists (e.g., 0.26 between Detailed Research Plan and Training & Development), which prevents a perfect score for af.2.a and af.2.c.
sub_criteria.rationale: While duplication is minimal, some lexical overlap between sections exists (e.g., 0.26 between Detailed Research Plan and Training & Development), which prevents a perfect score for af.2.a and af.2.c.

sub_criteria.name: Logical Flow and Coherence Across Sections
sub_criteria.definition: The application progresses logically, with a clear thread linking the research question, objectives, and work packages.

signals.signal_text: Semantic coherence between sections (using embeddings or similarity metrics)
Explicit linking of objectives to methods and work packages
Presence of transitional phrases or linking statements
Detectable narrative thread anchored on research aims

sub_criteria.pros: The application exhibits strong logical flow with 42 transition phrases and 192 objective-method-budget link terms. Work packages are explicitly linked to objectives (e.g., systematic review, meta-analysis, qualitative interviews). Section order progresses logically from applicant background to dissemination.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Semantic coherence between sections is inferred from lexical overlaps and structure rather than direct coherence metrics; signal af.3.a is strong but not perfectly demonstrated. The narrative thread is clear but could be more explicitly stated.
sub_criteria.rationale: Semantic coherence between sections is inferred from lexical overlaps and structure rather than direct coherence metrics; signal af.3.a is strong but not perfectly demonstrated. The narrative thread is clear but could be more explicitly stated.
